extraction.one_line_summary: O'CONNOR Ben attacked with 8km remaining on Madonna di Campiglio's final climb, soloing from a 19-rider break to take his first Grand Tour stage win.
tactical_patterns: Movistar placed four riders in 19-man break, revealing resource commitment strategy to defend mountain jersey ahead of GC support.
PERNSTEINER Hermann chased as most dangerous GC-positioned breakaway rider, nearly 10 minutes down, exploiting threshold for GC team intervention.
HINDLEY Jai attacked from GC group but failed, exposing Sunweb's reduced firepower compared to previous mountain stages.
ALMEIDA João's group remained static despite 17-second maglia rosa margin, suggesting GC contenders conserving energy before Stelvio stage.

rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: Second day in breakaway after previous runner-up finish, then attacked with 8km out showing exceptional multi-day breakaway recovery.
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: negative: Solo attack from GC group failed completely, suggesting insufficient form or team support entering decisive Stelvio stage.
rider_signals.PERNSTEINER Hermann: positive: Most dangerous breakaway threat despite 10-minute deficit, indicating strong climbing form and GC teams' calculation thresholds.
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: neutral: Remained fourth in GC but showed no aggression with Stelvio approaching, unclear if conserving or unable to attack.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: Sunweb unable to pressure 17-second deficit despite reduced team strength being explicitly noted as tactical limitation.
